region_of_origin: Mexico / United States (adapted for Singapore market)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic taco entry; no prawn-specific taco entry exists. Mapped to closest parent class.
ontology.foodex2_description: Crustaceans and products thereof — composite dish
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No exact FoodEx2 code for prawn tacos; mapped to crustacean-based composite dish as closest defensible anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for prawn tacos as a composite dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of prawn tacos consists of two soft tortillas filled with seasoned prawns, shredded cabbage, salsa, and a drizzle of sour cream or lime crema, weighing approximately 200 g.

nutrient_source.title: Prawn Tacos — HPB Singapore Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Prawn Tacos (cr_id G00010826) with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat and sugar values estimated from USDA FoodData Central composite prawn taco profile (USDA SR Legacy #35196 shrimp + tortilla composite) where HPB does not report these sub-fractions.

gi_source.title: Soft flour tortilla and mixed seafood composite — analogous GI estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for soft wheat tortilla (GI ~52) and prawn/shrimp (negligible GI contribution)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for prawn tacos. GI estimated at 52 based on the dominant carbohydrate vehicle: soft wheat flour tortilla (GI ~52, University of Sydney GI Database). Prawns and vegetable toppings contribute negligible GI. Glycemic load calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (52 × 30.2) / 100 ≈ 15.7 for a 200 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium at 363 mg/100 g is borderline-high; potassium at 229 mg/100 g partially offsets this via the Na:K ratio
hypertension.guidance: Individuals managing hypertension should limit portion size to one taco and request low-sodium seasoning, as the 363 mg/100 g sodium content approaches the threshold for a high-sodium food despite partial mitigation from 229 mg/100 g potassium per DASH diet principles.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate carbohydrate load (15.1 g/100 g) with low GI (~52) and 3.0 g/100 g dietary fibre supporting attenuated postprandial glucose response
type2_diabetes.guidance: Prawn tacos have a low estimated GI of 52 and 3.0 g/100 g dietary fibre, which together moderate postprandial glycaemia; individuals with type 2 diabetes should prefer corn tortillas over flour tortillas for a further GI reduction and limit added sour cream to control caloric density.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Sodium at 363 mg/100 g is the primary cardiovascular concern; total fat 9.17 g/100 g with estimated saturated fat ~2.8 g/100 g is within acceptable limits; prawns provide cardioprotective omega-3 fatty acids
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For cardiovascular disease management, prawn tacos offer beneficial omega-3 fatty acids from prawns and a moderate saturated fat level (~2.8 g/100 g), but the sodium content of 363 mg/100 g warrants portion control and low-sodium preparation to remain within the AHA recommended daily sodium limit.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Omega-3 fatty acids from prawns and anti-inflammatory fibre at 3.0 g/100 g; nightshade ingredients (tomato, chilli) may aggravate symptoms in sensitive individuals
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Prawn tacos are generally suitable for rheumatoid arthritis due to omega-3 content and low saturated fat (~2.8 g/100 g), but individuals sensitive to nightshades should monitor tomato-based salsa and chilli toppings as solanine may aggravate RA flares.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Prawn Tacos are a Western-style seafood dish consisting of seasoned prawns (shrimp) served in soft tortillas, typically accompanied by shredded cabbage, tomato salsa, lime juice, and sour cream or lime crema. The dish is popular in Singapore as a casual lunch or dinner option and is adapted from Mexican-American taco traditions. Prawns are usually grilled, pan-fried, or sautéed with garlic, cumin, and chilli before being placed in warm flour or corn tortillas. The texture profile combines the tender, slightly springy bite of cooked prawns with the soft chew of the tortilla and the crunch of fresh cabbage. Nutritionally, prawn tacos provide 170 kcal per 100 g, with 6.69 g protein, 9.17 g fat, and 15.1 g carbohydrates. Dietary fibre at 3.0 g/100 g supports digestive health and moderates glycaemic response. Sodium at 363 mg/100 g is borderline-high and warrants attention for hypertensive individuals. The estimated glycemic index of 52 (low) makes this dish suitable for blood glucose management when portion-controlled. Prawns contribute omega-3 fatty acids with cardioprotective and anti-inflammatory properties. The dish is halal-friendly and can be made gluten-free by substituting corn tortillas.
common_user_queries: How many calories in prawn tacos?
Are prawn tacos high in sodium?
What is the GI of prawn tacos?
Are prawn tacos suitable for hypertension?

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010826) with a stated confidence of 0.95. Saturated fat, sugar, monounsaturated fat, and polyunsaturated fat sub-fractions estimated from USDA FoodData Central composite prawn taco profile as HPB does not report these sub-fractions. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous entry for soft wheat tortilla.
notes: Saturated fat (2.8 g/100 g), sugar (2.5 g/100 g), monounsaturated fat (3.5 g/100 g), and polyunsaturated fat (1.9 g/100 g) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy composite prawn/shrimp taco profile where HPB does not report these sub-fractions. Trans fat set to 0.0 g as confirmed negligible in USDA SR Legacy shrimp taco composite. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed micronutrient and moisture data for this composite prawn taco preparation are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as a matched composite entry as of 2026. GI of 52 is an analogous estimate based on the soft wheat flour tortilla entry in the University of Sydney GI Database, as no direct GI measurement for prawn tacos exists in any approved GI source. Glycemic load of 15.7 calculated for a 200 g serving (30.2 g carbohydrate). Halal status assumed based on standard prawn taco preparation without pork-derived ingredients; kosher status not confirmed. Nightshade caution noted for rheumatoid arthritis sensitive individuals.
